title: PublicSchema External System Matching
description: Documents how an external system's concepts, vocabularies, and properties align with PublicSchema. One file per system at external/<system>/matching.yaml. The build pipeline projects this into dist/system_matchings.json which the site consumes.

system.description: snake_case system identifier. Must match a key in site/src/data/systems.ts systemRegistry.

system_version.description: Version of the external system the matching was performed against (e.g. '19.0', 'v2-event-service', 'stable-2-42-4').

source_repository.description: Authoritative source the matching was derived from (typically a GitHub URL).

source_branch.description: Branch or tag name within source_repository.

fhir_repository.description: Optional secondary FHIR repository (used by openimis where FHIR mappings live in a separate repo).

country_config_reference.description: Optional reference to a country configuration repository (used by opencrvs where many vocabularies are country-configured rather than core).

last_reviewed.description: ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) when the matching was last verified against the external system. Surfaced on the site as a freshness indicator.

concept_matches.description: Concept-level alignments between PublicSchema concepts and external system entities. Empty array if not yet researched.

matches.description: Vocabulary- and property-level matches. May include code-by-code value_mapping tables, same_standard flags, and gap notes.

no_match.description: PublicSchema concepts, vocabularies, or properties that the external system does not model, with a reason. Each entry MUST reference a v2_concept, v2_vocabulary, or v2_property; entries describing external-only items belong in external_excess.

external_excess.description: External system fields, vocabularies, or entities that PublicSchema does not currently model, with a reason. This is a PublicSchema coverage backlog signal, not a system-coverage signal, and is intentionally not surfaced on system detail pages.

match_level.description: Strength of alignment. exact = identical scope and codes; close = same scope, minor differences; broad = external is broader than PublicSchema; narrow = external is narrower; related = adjacent but not directly substitutable.

confidence.description: Reviewer confidence in the match. Independent of match_level: a 'related' match can be high-confidence.

v2_concept.description: PublicSchema concept reference. Domain-scoped concepts use composite form (e.g. 'sp/Enrollment', 'crvs/Birth'); root concepts use bare id (e.g. 'Person').

external_entity.description: Name or identifier of the corresponding external entity (class, resource, model).

external_source.description: Path or URL pointing at the external definition.

surface.description: Where in the external system the entity is exposed (e.g. 'rest_api_v2', 'event_v2', 'internal_model'). Useful when a system distinguishes a public API surface from internal models.

notes.description: Free-text rationale, caveats, or shape differences.

decomposition.description: Optional field-by-field projection, used when a single external entity decomposes into multiple PublicSchema concepts.

v2_vocabulary.description: PublicSchema vocabulary reference (canonical '<domain>/<id>' or bare '<id>' form).

same_standard.description: Both sides reference the same international standard (e.g. ISO 4217 currencies). Implies value-by-value equivalence without an explicit value_mapping.

value_mapping.description: Code-by-code map. Keys are external codes, values are PublicSchema codes (or null when no PublicSchema equivalent exists).

gaps.description: Codes or aspects present on one side but not the other. Two accepted shapes: (a) an object with v2_only/external_only code lists (used by most systems for vocabulary mappings); (b) an array of free-text gap descriptions (used by ocha-cods for property-level mappings where gaps are prose, not enumerable codes).

fhir_resource.description: openimis-specific: the FHIR resource the vocabulary is exposed through.

reason.description: Why the external system does not model this PublicSchema item. Surfaced on the system detail page.
Why PublicSchema does not currently model this external item. Feeds the PublicSchema coverage backlog, not the public system page.
